audit_method: Enriched walkability audit with pedestrian infrastructure, safety, transit connectivity, and OSM road data analysis

sidewalk_quality.details: Continuous concrete sidewalks on NW 23rd Avenue, NW Lovejoy Street, NW Northrup Street, and all cross streets. Well-maintained with no significant gaps or obstructions.
sidewalk_quality.obstructions: Minimal - occasional street furniture but well-managed

curb_ramps.ada_compliance: Yes - curb ramps at all signalized intersections per ADA standard

pedestrian_crossings.marked_crosswalks: All signalized intersections have marked crosswalks
pedestrian_crossings.transparent_crosswalks: Yes - Portland-style transparent crosswalks at some intersections
pedestrian_crossings.crossing_cues: Audible pedestrian signals at major crossings for visually impaired

street_lighting.rating: Good - Portland streetlights along all blocks
street_lighting.type: Modern LED streetlights on NW 23rd and cross streets

wayfinding.street_signs: Standard Portland street signs at all intersections
wayfinding.neighborhood_signage: Nob Hill neighborhood signs at entry points on NW 23rd

traffic_volume.nw_23rd_ave: Low-Moderate - residential/commercial mix, much pedestrian traffic compensates

traffic_calming.speed_humps: No speed humps directly on NW 23rd, but present on nearby residential streets (NW Irving, NW Lovejoy)

safety_assessment.pedestrian_conflicts: Low - wide sidewalks, slow speeds, signalized crossings
safety_assessment.bicycle_pedestrian_conflicts: Low - bike lanes on adjacent streets separate bikes from pedestrians on NW 23rd
safety_assessment.night_safety: Good - street lighting, active commercial corridor, continuous eyes on the street

noise_environmental_factors.noise_level: Low-Moderate - NW 23rd is quieter than Burnside corridor
noise_environmental_factors.air_quality: Good - low traffic density, tree-lined street
noise_environmental_factors.green_space_proximity: Excellent - Nob Hill Nature Park and pocket parks within walking distance

cycle_tracks.NW_21st_St: Class III cycle track (separated bike lane) - 2 blocks south
cycle_tracks.NW_18th_St: Class III cycle track (separated bike lane) - 4 blocks south
cycle_tracks.NW_23rd_ave: Shared lane markings (sharrows) - not a protected bike lane but wide and navigable

bike_lanes_connectivity.north_south: Protected cycle tracks on NW 21st and NW 18th provide north-south connectivity
bike_lanes_connectivity.east_west: Bike lanes on Burnside St and NW Irving connect to downtown and NW Heights
bike_lanes_connectivity.downtown_connection: NW 23rd to Burnside to downtown (3km, bike-friendly)

bicycle_infrastructure_audit.bike_parking: Bike racks at street corners along NW 23rd and cross streets

bike_share.availability: Stations nearby at NW 23rd & Marshall area

historic_cultural_context.neighborhood_history: Part of Portland's Nob Hill (Homestead) neighborhood - one of the city's earliest developed residential districts (early 1900s)
historic_cultural_context.heritage_tree: Portland Heritage Tree #10 on NW 23rd Avenue
historic_cultural_context.architectural_character: Historic homes mixed with modern renovations; NW 23rd Ave has a Village Center character with independent shops and restaurants

ada_compliance.pedestrian_signals: Audible pedestrian signals at major crossings
ada_compliance.restrooms: Not available within 1km - Nearest public restrooms at Forest Park or NW 23rd corridor
ada_compliance.seating: Benches available at Nob Hill Nature Park, NW Northrup corridor

accessibility_features.walkability_for_elderly: Excellent - flat terrain, wide sidewalks, frequent crossings, short distances to daily needs
accessibility_features.walkability_for_children: Very Good - parks nearby, pedestrian-friendly streets, but limited within-supervision playgrounds in immediate 1km

data_verification.verified_by: Enriched live analysis via OSM tools (geocoding, area exploration, nearby places, transport routing, EV charging stations)
